name: Tuva (Russia)

colors.symbolism: courage, strength and the eternal blue sky of the Tuvan homeland
the purity of the rivers (the Bii-Khem and Kaa-Khem, sources of the Yenisei) and high moral values
Buddhist faith, wealth and prosperity of the Tuvan people

description: A light-blue field with a white wedge issuing from the hoist that splits into two diagonal stripes meeting at the fly, dividing the field into upper, central and lower zones; a yellow disc is placed in the upper canton near the hoist.
history: The flag of the Republic of Tuva was adopted on 17 September 1992. The diverging white stripes symbolise the meeting of the Bii-Khem (Greater Yenisei) and Kaa-Khem (Lesser Yenisei) rivers, which join in Tuva to form the great Yenisei river.

features.symbolism: a light-blue field divided by a white wedge issuing from the hoist that splits into two diagonal arms; a yellow disc sits in the canton above. The pattern alludes to the confluence of the two source rivers of the Yenisei in Tuva.
represents the sun and Buddhist tradition; placed in the upper canton formed by the white wedges.
